claims.predicate: In this work, as in [7], local updates in batches are counted rather than local epochs, aligning with theoretical formulations and extending to related strategies based on FedAvg.
claims.notes: Generated via strict guide -> atomic -> filter pipeline aligned with paper_fingerprint_schema.md

claims.predicate: Client updates are computed as: $$ \begin{array}{l} \Delta_ {t} = \frac {1}{| S |} \sum_ {i \in S} \Delta_ {i} ^ {t} \\ m _ {t} = \beta_ {1} m _ {t - 1} + (1 - \beta_ {1}) \Delta_ {t} \tag {1} \\ v _ {t} = \beta_ {2} v _ {t - 1} + (1 - \beta_ {2}) (\Delta_ {t}) ^ {2} \\ x _ {t + 1} = x _ {t} - \eta \frac {m _ {t}}{\sqrt {v _ {t}} + \tau} \\ \end{array} $$ where $\Delta _ { t }$ is the aggregated gradient update at time t, $S$ is the set of clients participating, $| S |$ is the number of clients in $S$ , $m _ { t }$ and $v _ { t }$ are the first and second moment estimates, $\beta _ { 1 }$ and $\beta _ { 2 }$ are the exponential decay rates for these moment estimates, $\eta$ is the learning rate, and $\tau$ is a small constant for numerical stability.
claims.notes: Generated via strict guide -> atomic -> filter pipeline aligned with paper_fingerprint_schema.md

claims.predicate: Updates for the global and local control variables and model parameters are computed as follows: $$ \begin{array}{l} \boldsymbol {y} _ {i} \leftarrow \boldsymbol {y} _ {i} - \eta_ {l} \left(g _ {i} \left(\boldsymbol {y} _ {i}\right) + \boldsymbol {c} - \boldsymbol {c} _ {i}\right) \\ c _ {i} ^ {+} \leftarrow (\mathrm {i}) g _ {i} (\boldsymbol {x}), \text {o r (i i)} \boldsymbol {c} _ {i} - \boldsymbol {c} + \frac {1}{K \eta_ {l}} (\boldsymbol {x} - \boldsymbol {y} _ {i}) \\ \boldsymbol {x} \leftarrow \boldsymbol {x} + \frac {\eta_ {g}}{| S |} \sum_ {i \in S} (\boldsymbol {y} _ {i} - \boldsymbol {x}) \\ \boldsymbol {c} \gets \boldsymbol {c} + \frac {1}{N} \sum_ {i \in S} (\boldsymbol {c} _ {\boldsymbol {i}} ^ {+} - \boldsymbol {c} _ {\boldsymbol {i}}) \\ \end{array} $$ where $\mathbf { { \mathit { y } } } _ { i }$ is client update performed $K$ times, $\eta$ is the learning rate, $g _ { i } ( y _ { i } )$ is the mini-batch gradient, and $_ { \textbf { \em x } }$ are the server parameters.
claims.notes: Generated via strict guide -> atomic -> filter pipeline aligned with paper_fingerprint_schema.md
